Clicking or tapping a category pulls the gaze downward into the corresponding FAQ section panel and then left-to-right across individual question rows. URL-based section navigation and any sticky behavior on the FAQ nav ensure that as users follow links or scroll, their visual focus remains clearly anchored to the current section.

layout.overall_description: Below the site’s existing utility bar, logo/navigation header, and bright full-width hero, the Policies & FAQs page content begins inside a centered, max-width container that tracks the implicit 12-column grid. The first band is a simple, clean title-and-intro area: “Policies & FAQs” appears left-aligned or slightly offset from center with large, friendly typography, followed by a short, welcoming paragraph explaining how to use the page. A comfortable 32–48px gap separates this intro from the main policies band below. The policies band spans roughly 70–80% of the viewport width on desktop, with generous left and right margins, and reads like an editorial article: clear subheadings for pool rules, safety, memberships, etc., stacked one after another with ample whitespace and large, readable line spacing. After another clear vertical break, the FAQ band appears. At its top is a full-width horizontal FAQ section nav—a row of colorful yet clean category pills or tabs that invite scanning left-to-right, echoing the mockup’s horizontal hero cards. This nav sits just above the FAQ content and may subtly stick to the top of the viewport as you scroll deeper into the FAQs. Below it, the FAQ sections themselves form a vertical stack of panels, each with its own heading and a list of question rows. Each question is an accordion: a padded row with the question text and an icon, which expands to reveal a comfortably spaced answer. Across the page, the white background and clear vertical rhythm create a calm, orderly reading experience, while the nav elements and accent colors keep the tone bright and family-friendly. Invisible URL-sync logic ensures that when users follow deep links or change FAQ categories, the scroll position and active section remain tightly aligned with the visual layout.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The page controller anchors all Policies & FAQs content just below the global hero section, inside a centered max-width container that follows the implicit 12-column grid. On desktop, it constrains the main content to roughly 1120–1200px width and centers it within the viewport, leaving generous white margins on both sides. Vertically, it organizes the content into three primary stacked bands: (1) a page-intro band with the page title and short description at the top of the content area, (2) a policies band, and (3) an FAQ band containing the in-page nav and FAQ sections. It applies consistent vertical spacing between these bands, using ~32–48px gaps to echo the mockup’s section separation. Within the bands, it enforces a single, central content column that roughly spans 8–10 of the 12 columns, aligning section headings, body text, and FAQ elements along a shared left baseline, which supports a smooth vertical reading flow. On smaller screens, it collapses naturally to a single-column layout with 16–24px side padding, maintaining the same stacked order and breathing room. It also reserves the lower-left area of the viewport for a floating chat button (part of the global layout), ensuring that this page’s content doesn’t conflict with that element’s safe area.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Acts as the main flow container for the page, participating in normal document flow. It manages anchor offsets for in-page navigation and URL-based jumps (ensuring scroll positions account for sticky or fixed items like the FAQ nav and global header) but itself is not fixed or sticky.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The policies-content group forms the main policies band and sits directly below the page-intro band, separated by roughly 32–40px of whitespace. It spans the full width of the central content column defined by the page controller—on desktop this is about 70–80% of the viewport width, centered, effectively using 8–10 of 12 columns. Within this area, the content is arranged as a vertical stack of policy sections (e.g., Pool Policies, Safety Guidelines, Membership Terms), each beginning with a clear, left-aligned subheading followed by one or more paragraphs or bullet lists. Each subsection uses internal vertical spacing of about 16–24px between the heading and the first paragraph, and about 12–16px between paragraphs or bullet clusters, preserving a low-to-medium density feel. The line length for text is kept comfortable (around 60–75 characters per line) with large, readable line height, so the block feels open despite potentially heavy content. On wide screens, some structured details (like short lists of rules) may use two-column lists within the same central column (splitting into ~6/12 + 6/12) if needed, but they never break the feeling of a single, cohesive central content column. The bottom edge of this group is clearly separated from the upcoming FAQ nav by another 32–48px gap, visually signaling the transition from policy reference material to interactive Q&A.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows naturally as a tall text block within the document. It stretches vertically according to content length, with internal anchors marking key policy sections for deep linking. It does not exhibit sticky or fixed behavior, but its top and internal headings are designed to be targetable by URL-based navigation.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The FAQ section nav is the gateway into the FAQ band and is positioned immediately below the policies-content group, with a distinct 32–40px buffer of whitespace above it. It spans the full width of the central content column and appears as a low-profile but visually engaging horizontal strip: a row of category pills or tab-like buttons (e.g., General, Membership, Swim Lessons, Safety) arranged left-to-right. Each nav item is sized to its label and padded generously (approximately 12–16px vertically and 16–24px horizontally), creating large, tappable targets that mirror the generous padding rules from the mockup. The row aligns with the same left baseline as the policies headings but visually feels like a simple horizontal selector, echoing the mockup’s pattern of scanning across a band of hero cards. The active category is highlighted with a bright, family-friendly accent color and possibly a subtle pill background, while inactive items use a softer or outlined style. The nav strip itself sits on a white background but may have a subtle bottom border or shadow to visually separate it from the FAQ panels below. On mobile, the nav items can horizontally scroll within the same band while retaining their padding and left alignment, ensuring the nav still feels like a unified horizontal control.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Participates in normal flow initially, but on desktop and medium screens it becomes sticky once it reaches the top edge of the main content area: as the user scrolls into the FAQ band, the nav pins to the top of the viewport (just below the global header) while FAQ panels scroll beneath it. On small screens, it may either remain non-sticky or switch to a simpler sticky behavior to avoid vertical crowding. It also visually reflects the current section based on URL/query changes, remaining anchored in place while content below changes or scrolls into view.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The FAQ section list lives directly beneath the FAQ section nav, with a modest 16–24px gap separating the two. It occupies the same central content column width, maintaining alignment with both the nav items above and the policies content above that. The list is structured as a vertical stack of FAQ section panels—one per category or topic. Each panel’s outer width matches the content column, and panels are separated from each other by about 24–32px of vertical space. On a light background, each panel can be subtly card-like (white card on a slightly tinted page background, or a faint border) to visually group questions within a category without feeling heavy. As the user selects a category in the nav, the list either scrolls the corresponding section panel to the top (just beneath the sticky nav) or visually emphasizes that panel while leaving others collapsed or further down the page. The list height is variable and can extend well beyond the fold, but the repeated vertical rhythm of equally spaced panels keeps the layout coherent as the user scrolls.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows in the normal document stream beneath the FAQ nav. It coordinates with URL-based and nav-based navigation to scroll specific section panels into view, typically aligning the top of the targeted panel just below the sticky FAQ nav. It does not stick or float itself; its behavior is to expand vertically with the number and size of FAQ panels.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each faq-section-panel is a self-contained block inside the FAQ section list, spanning the full width of the central content column. At the top of each panel is a left-aligned section heading (e.g., “Membership FAQs”), given strong typographic emphasis and separated from the panel’s upper edge by about 16–24px of padding. Surrounding the panel, there is an internal padding of approximately 24px on desktop (16–20px on mobile), providing a card-like feel without making it heavy. Below the heading, the panel contains a vertical list of faq-question-item rows, each separated by a subtle divider line or 8–16px of vertical spacing. The questions align left, with their text and icons lining up along the same vertical grid as the section heading, preserving a clean columnar structure. The bottom of the panel includes a bit of extra padding (16–24px) before transitioning to the next panel’s top margin, maintaining continuity in spacing. The visual density inside the panel is moderate—many questions can fit—but the padding and clear typographic hierarchy prevent it from feeling cramped.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Resides in normal flow as a block-level element. It may respond to routing and nav selection by being scrolled into position just under the FAQ nav and briefly emphasized (e.g., via a subtle highlight or focus management), but it does not have sticky behavior. Its height changes dynamically as faq-question-items expand or collapse within it.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each faq-question-item appears as a full-width row within its parent faq-section-panel, occupying the entire width of the content column within that panel’s padding. The default (collapsed) state shows a question header bar: the question text aligned to the left with a clear, readable type size, and a chevron or plus/minus icon aligned to the right. This header row has generous vertical padding (about 12–16px top and bottom) and horizontal padding that lines up with the panel’s internal padding, ensuring the question text does not touch the card edges. A subtle divider line or bottom border helps visually separate adjacent questions. When expanded, the row grows downward to reveal the answer text directly below the question header, indented or aligned to the same left baseline, with about 8–12px of space between the question and the answer. The answer content uses the same typographic scale as policy body text, with comfortable line-height and short paragraphs to remain easy to skim. On touch devices, the entire row (not just the icon) is tappable, consistent with the mockup’s generous interactive areas. Multiple questions can be open at once without breaking the vertical rhythm; the panel simply elongates to accommodate the expanded answers.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Behaves like an accordion item in vertical flow: it expands and collapses smoothly, pushing content below it up or down. It does not float or stick. It can respond to URL/query parameters by auto-expanding a specific question and ensuring it is scrolled into view beneath the sticky FAQ nav when directly linked.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each faq-section-panel is a self-contained block inside the FAQ section list, spanning the full width of the central content column. At the top of each panel is a left-aligned section heading (e.g., “Membership FAQs”), given strong typographic emphasis and separated from the panel’s upper edge by about 16–24px of padding. Surrounding the panel, there is an internal padding of approximately 24px on desktop (16–20px on mobile), providing a card-like feel without making it heavy. Below the heading, the panel contains a vertical list of faq-question-item rows, each separated by a subtle divider line or 8–16px of vertical spacing. The questions align left, with their text and icons lining up along the same vertical grid as the section heading, preserving a clean columnar structure. The bottom of the panel includes a bit of extra padding (16–24px) before transitioning to the next panel’s top margin, maintaining continuity in spacing. The visual density inside the panel is moderate—many questions can fit—but the padding and clear typographic hierarchy prevent it from feeling cramped.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Resides in normal flow as a block-level element. It may respond to routing and nav selection by being scrolled into position just under the FAQ nav and briefly emphasized (e.g., via a subtle highlight or focus management), but it does not have sticky behavior. Its height changes dynamically as faq-question-items expand or collapse within it.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The url-query-sync component exists as a non-visual helper that underpins the deep-linking behavior of the Policies & FAQs page. It has no visible footprint and occupies no spatial area in the layout; instead, it conceptually sits at the page-controller level, listening for and updating URL query parameters or hash fragments. When a user lands on a URL that targets a specific policy section, FAQ category, or individual question, this helper interprets the URL, activates the corresponding navigation state (e.g., selects the right FAQ tab, expands the right question), and instructs the page to scroll so that the target element is aligned just below any fixed or sticky headers (global header and FAQ nav). Similarly, when users change sections via the FAQ nav or open a specific question, it updates the URL to reflect the current state without disrupting the smooth vertical flow.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Purely behavioral and non-visual; it does not participate in layout, have size, or affect stacking context. Its effect is indirect: it triggers scrolling and state changes in visual components so that the layout and URL remain synchronized.
